content:
Hi! So today I won the bug lottery and also managed to discover a bug where if I attempt to build this kernel, rustc just hangs. I guess I was wondering if we have anyone here who triages issues like this or at least knows how I could turn a kernel branch like this into an example of whatever is hanging the compiler when I try to build this, so I can actually file a bug for it upstream:

https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/lyudess/linux/-/tree/rust/rust-stuck?ref_type=heads

(Also, I'm kind of generally stuck since I can't build it without the compiler freezing, heh)
Do you know where it's stuck? If not, could you try running rustc under a debugger to capture a backtrace while it's stuck?
there's two other threads as well but neither of them looked relevant

I guess I was wondering if we have anyone here who triages issues like this or at least knows how I could turn a kernel branch like this into an example of whatever is hanging the compiler when I try to build this

I try to track toolchain related stuff in our usual lists from #2. We never had the compiler hanging from what I recall, but we have definitely hit e.g. ICEs in the past, and issues generally get fixed when we report them upstream.

I can reproduce the hang -- using the new trait solver on kernel gives a hint:

     error[E0283]: type annotations needed
        --> rust/kernel/drm/device.rs:232:22
         |
     232 |         fbdev_probe: T::Object::ALLOC_OPS.fbdev_probe,
         |                      ^^^^^^^^^ cannot infer type for type parameter `Ctx` declared on the associated type `Object`
         |
         = note: the type must implement `drm::device::DeviceContext`

And with the following the hang does not happen anymore:

diff --git a/rust/kernel/drm/device.rs b/rust/kernel/drm/device.rs
index 9641102bd87f..a32c79dfd6e8 100644
--- a/rust/kernel/drm/device.rs
+++ b/rust/kernel/drm/device.rs
@@ -229,7 +229,7 @@ const fn compute_features() -> u32 {
         dumb_map_offset: T::Object::<Uninit>::ALLOC_OPS.dumb_map_offset,

         show_fdinfo: None,
-        fbdev_probe: T::Object::ALLOC_OPS.fbdev_probe,
+        fbdev_probe: T::Object::<Uninit>::ALLOC_OPS.fbdev_probe,

         major: T::INFO.major,
         minor: T::INFO.minor,

I hope that helps!

This hangs it:

trait Bound {}

trait Alloc {
    const OPS: u32;
}

trait Driver {
    type Object<Ctx: Bound>: Alloc;
}

fn f<T: Driver>() -> u32 {
    T::Object::OPS
}

which one can easily see in e.g. Compiler Explorer. It may be one of the existing issues they have, but I will fill it anyway.
